text: Literally, ‘the mountain’, but it is evident from the context that the narrative is now resumed where it was left off in 9.21.

It is not clear whether we are to understand that Moses put the stone tablets in a chest specially made to contain them until the tabernacle should be built, or whether (as verse 5 seems to imply) the Ark itself is here meant. If so, the historical order of events is given somewhat confusedly. Cf. Ex. 34.1; 37.1.
vv. 6-7 seem to be a note, taken from some record of the wanderings, which is perhaps put in here to illustrate 9.20 above.
